data.title: AstraZeneca’s New Hope for NSCLC Patients: Datopotamab Deruxtecan Access Program
data.description:
AstraZeneca ((AZN)), AstraZeneca plc ((GB:AZN)), AstraZeneca ((DE:ZEGA)), AstraZeneca plc US ((AZNCF)) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Don’t Miss TipRanks’ Half-Year Sale

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.
  • Make smarter investment decisions with TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks, delivered to your inbox every week.

Study Overview: The Medical Access Program for Datopotamab Deruxtecan (Dato-DXd, DS-1062a) aims to provide access to this drug for patients with advanced or metastatic EGFR mutated non-small cell lung cancer (NSCLC) who have exhausted other treatment options. This program addresses the unmet clinical needs of these patients, offering a potential alternative where existing treatments fall short.

Intervention/Treatment: The intervention being tested is Datopotamab Deruxtecan, administered as a 6 mg/kg intravenous infusion every three weeks. It is designed to treat patients with advanced NSCLC who have specific genetic mutations.

Study Design: This is an Expanded Access study, meaning it provides the drug to patients outside of a traditional clinical trial setting. There is no randomization or masking involved, and the primary purpose is to offer treatment to those in need.

Study Timeline: The study was first submitted on February 19, 2024, and the latest update was submitted on June 30, 2025. These dates are crucial as they mark the progression and current status of the program, indicating its ongoing nature.

Market Implications: The approval for marketing of Dato-DXd could positively impact AstraZeneca and Daiichi Sankyo’s stock performance, as it addresses a significant unmet need in the NSCLC treatment landscape. This development could also influence investor sentiment positively, especially in comparison to competitors who may not have similar offerings.

The study is ongoing, with further details available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

data.title: Incyte’s Phase 3 Study Update: Potential Market Impact for HNSCC Treatment
data.description:
Incyte Corporation (($CC:INCY.CUR)) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Don’t Miss TipRanks’ Half-Year Sale

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.
  • Make smarter investment decisions with TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks, delivered to your inbox every week.

Incyte Corporation, in collaboration with Merck Sharp & Dohme LLC, is conducting a Phase 3 clinical study titled A Phase 3 Randomized, Open-Label Clinical Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Pembrolizumab Plus Epacadostat, Pembrolizumab Monotherapy, and the EXTREME Regimen as First Line Treatment for Recurrent or Metastatic Head and Neck Squamous Cell Carcinoma (KEYNOTE-669/ECHO-304). The study aims to assess the efficacy and safety of three treatment regimens for recurrent or metastatic head and neck squamous cell carcinoma (HNSCC).

The interventions being tested include Pembrolizumab plus Epacadostat, Pembrolizumab monotherapy, and the EXTREME regimen, which consists of cetuximab combined with cisplatin or carboplatin and 5-fluorouracil. These treatments are designed to improve outcomes for patients with HNSCC.

The study follows an interventional design with randomized allocation and a parallel intervention model. It is open-label, meaning no masking is involved, and its primary purpose is treatment-focused.

The study began on December 1, 2017, with primary completion and results first submitted in July 2019. The latest update was submitted in July 2025, indicating ongoing data collection and analysis.

This study update could influence Incyte Corporation’s stock performance by potentially enhancing investor confidence if the results show significant efficacy and safety improvements. The competitive landscape includes other pharmaceutical companies developing treatments for HNSCC, which could impact market dynamics.

The study is currently active but not recruiting, with further details available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

data.title: Medtronic’s EnTRINSIC Study Update: Implications for Cardiac Care and Market Dynamics
data.description:
Medtronic ((MDT)) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Don’t Miss TipRanks’ Half-Year Sale

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.
  • Make smarter investment decisions with TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks, delivered to your inbox every week.

The EnTRINSIC study, officially titled ‘EnPulse Trial on Search AV+ Influence,’ was initiated by Medtronic Cardiac Rhythm and Heart Failure to evaluate the effects of the Search AV+ algorithm in pacemakers. The study aims to assess ventricular stimulation, hospitalizations, atrial fibrillation occurrences, and drug usage in patients with pacemakers using the Search AV+ algorithm compared to those with optimized pacemaker settings. This research is significant as it addresses the potential adverse effects of unnecessary right ventricular apical pacing in patients with stable atrioventricular conduction, particularly those with additional cardiac conditions like heart failure.

The intervention tested in this study is the Search AV+ algorithm, a device designed to minimize right ventricular pacing and support intrinsic AV-conduction, thereby potentially reducing the negative impacts associated with ventricular pacing.

The study follows an interventional design with a randomized allocation and a parallel intervention model. It is a Phase 4 trial with the primary purpose of treatment, and there is no masking involved, meaning all participants and researchers are aware of the treatment being administered.

The study began in February 2004, with its primary completion and estimated completion dates not specified. The last update was submitted on June 30, 2025. These dates are crucial as they indicate the study’s progress and the timeline for potential results that could impact clinical practices and market dynamics.

The termination of the EnTRINSIC study could have mixed implications for Medtronic’s stock performance and investor sentiment. While the study’s termination might initially raise concerns, the ongoing updates suggest Medtronic’s commitment to refining its cardiac solutions. Investors should monitor competitor activities and industry trends, as advancements in cardiac pacing technologies continue to evolve.

The study is currently terminated, with further details available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

data.title: AstraZeneca’s Tezepelumab Study in Spain: Key Insights for Investors
data.description:
AstraZeneca ((AZN)), AstraZeneca plc ((GB:AZN)), AstraZeneca ((DE:ZEGA)), AstraZeneca plc US ((AZNCF)) announced an update on their ongoing clinical study.

Don’t Miss TipRanks’ Half-Year Sale

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.
  • Make smarter investment decisions with TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks, delivered to your inbox every week.

Study Overview: AstraZeneca is conducting an observational retrospective study titled ‘Observational Retrospective Study to Characterize and Assess Clinical Outcomes of Patients Receiving Tezepelumab After Marketing Approval in Spain.’ The study aims to evaluate the clinical outcomes of patients treated with tezepelumab, a medication approved for severe asthma, in routine clinical practice in Spain. This research is significant as it seeks to provide real-world evidence on the effectiveness of tezepelumab post-marketing.

Intervention/Treatment: The study focuses on tezepelumab, a treatment designed for patients with severe asthma. Tezepelumab is intended to help manage asthma symptoms and improve patient outcomes by targeting specific pathways involved in the disease.

Study Design: This is an observational, multi-centre, retrospective, single-arm study involving patients aged 12 and older with severe asthma. The study will include approximately 400 participants who meet the eligibility criteria. The observational model is case-only, and the time perspective is retrospective, meaning it looks back at existing data from patients already treated with tezepelumab.

Study Timeline: The study began on June 23, 2025, with the primary completion and estimated completion dates yet to be announced. The most recent update was submitted on July 1, 2025. These dates are crucial for tracking the progress and availability of results from the study.

Market Implications: This study update could positively influence AstraZeneca’s stock performance and investor sentiment by providing additional evidence of tezepelumab’s effectiveness in a real-world setting. As asthma treatments are a competitive market, demonstrating the drug’s success could enhance AstraZeneca’s position relative to competitors.

The study is currently ongoing, with further details available on the ClinicalTrials portal.

data.title: Greif Class A Divests Containerboard Business for $1.8 Billion
data.description:
Don’t Miss TipRanks’ Half-Year Sale

  • Take advantage of TipRanks Premium at 50% off! Unlock powerful investing tools, advanced data, and expert analyst insights to help you invest with confidence.
  • Make smarter investment decisions with TipRanks' Smart Investor Picks, delivered to your inbox every week.

Greif Class A ( (GEF) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On July 1, 2025, Greif, Inc. announced the divestiture of its Containerboard Business for $1.8 billion, marking a significant step in its strategic transformation. This move is part of Greif’s ‘Build to Last’ strategy, aimed at sharpening its portfolio and enhancing capital efficiency. The divestiture includes the company’s Containerboard Mills, CorrChoice sheet feeder network, and a box plant in North Carolina, but excludes its URB network and converting facilities. The transaction is expected to close by the end of the fiscal year, with proceeds used to reduce debt and position the company for future growth in high-margin packaging solutions. This strategic shift is intended to reduce exposure to low-growth markets and focus on areas with greater stability and growth potential, ultimately aiming for $1 billion EBITDA and $500 million Free Cash Flow by 2027.

The most recent analyst rating on (GEF) stock is a Buy with a $85.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Greif Class A stock, see the GEF Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on GEF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GEF is a Outperform.

Greif Class A’s overall stock score reflects strong earnings call results and solid financial performance, supported by effective cash flow management. Technical analysis suggests bullish momentum, though caution is warranted due to overbought signals. Valuation remains reasonable with a good dividend yield, although leverage poses some risk.

To see Spark’s full report on GEF stock, click here.

More about Greif Class A

Greif, Inc. operates in the industrial packaging industry, providing a range of products and services including containerboard solutions, packaging solutions, and related services. The company focuses on enhancing capital efficiency and advancing growth priorities, with a market focus on high-margin packaging solutions and polymer-based products.

Petroleo Brasileiro SA- Petrobras ( (PBR) ) has provided an update.

On July 3, 2025, Petrobras announced a substantial investment of approximately R$33 billion in refining and petrochemical projects in Rio de Janeiro. This includes the integration of the Boaventura Energy Complex and Reduc, aiming to enhance diesel, jet fuel, and lubricating oil production capacities. The projects are part of the 2025-2029 Business Plan, with a focus on modernization, energy efficiency, and decarbonization. The initiatives are expected to bolster Petrobras’ industry positioning and commitment to sustainable energy practices, with implications for increased production capacity and environmental responsibility.
